title: About Unit 02s Beast Mode
wikitext:
[]: About Unit 02['s Beast Mode]...             Quote Post by TK421 » Wed May 01, 2013 3:32 pm What was up with its Beast Mode in 3.33? It turned into a cat! That was really weird and seemed kinda out of place. Anyone have any ideas?

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Wed May 01, 2013 3:35 pm I guess anno was trying to be a little more literal on why it's called beast mode, after eva one really turned primal when it wen bezerk

[]:             Quote Post by thegoodson » Wed May 01, 2013 3:38 pm As a big fan of Q this was honestly the most jarring (but still oh das cool) aspects of 3. My best guess is its tied in with how 02 is the first "combat Eva" and has a different method of construction. Whether that involves giant panther DNA, idk.

[]: Re: [SPOILERS] About Unit 02...             Quote Post by qu4d » Wed May 01, 2013 3:41 pm TK421 wrote: Anyone have any ideas?

[]:             Quote Post by lastcody » Wed May 01, 2013 3:44 pm I think the beast mode form depends upon the user. or 3.0 Unit 02 might not be the same unit 02 we know

[]: Re: [SPOILERS] About Unit 02...             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Wed May 01, 2013 3:46 pm TK421 wrote: What was up with its Beast Mode in 3.33? It turned into a cat! That was really weird and seemed kinda out of place. Anyone have any ideas?

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Wed May 01, 2013 3:57 pm lastcody wrote: I think the beast mode form depends upon the user. or 3.0 Unit 02 might not be the same unit 02 we know

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed May 01, 2013 9:04 pm Lizard-dog from 2.0 is actually the second Beast Mode form. I'm guessing what we got prior to the kitty cat might be #1? Also, for those who might find it amusing: Eva-02 actually says "Nya" in the script.

[]:             Quote Post by TK421 » Wed May 01, 2013 9:08 pm I find it odd because, up until this point, the Evas have been primarily humanoid in appearance. Even when they go berserk they retain a fairly human shape, though they do crawl on all fours at times. But this? This is totally new. It has a tail. It has paws. It's just...weird. haha.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Wed May 01, 2013 9:13 pm Reichu wrote: Lizard-dog from 2.0 is actually the second Beast Mode form. I'm guessing what we got prior to the kitty cat might be #1? Also, for those who might find it amusing: Eva-02 actually says "Nya" in the script.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Wed May 01, 2013 9:15 pm Reichu wrote: Also, for those who might find it amusing: Eva-02 actually says "Nya" in the script.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed May 01, 2013 9:36 pm Has anyone here ever read the Book of Daniel in the Bible's Old Tentement? I always speculated that "Beast Mode" was a reference to God turning King Nebuchadnezzar into an animal. You can read that here: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/11639/The-Beast-Revisited/ Kitty Unit 02 doesn't look quite like the images in the Book of Daniel, but at least we can sort of confirm the Eva Unit growing some kind of fur this time around.

[]:             Quote Post by SSJ Spartan01 » Thu May 02, 2013 4:58 am I didn't find a whole lot wrong with Evangelion Q, but I'm afraid to say that Unit-02's Kitty Cat Beast Mode stage was probably the worst thing about the movie for me. I can't even imagine Anno coming up with this, it's just so random haha. There's a chance that this ascended mode of beast form could be due to Asuka's angel contamination.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Thu May 02, 2013 8:12 am ^ You're not wrong there - Everything about it screams Yamashita, haha.

[]:             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Thu May 02, 2013 8:15 am Obviously, in the NTE world, the pets of children are used to create Evangelions and not the souls of their mothers. "Going to live on the farm with grandpa" is actually code for becoming a giant robot to fight aliens. Obviously.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Thu May 02, 2013 8:28 am Well, Eva-01 did show some bestial behaviour in NGE, specifically when it got down on all fours and munched through Zeruel's carcass, although this is the first time an Eva's actually gone from humanoid to quadrupedal form...Possibly it has to do with the special code Asuka invoked?

[]:             Quote Post by TK421 » Thu May 02, 2013 10:57 am It just looks silly, like something out of another (inferior) giant robot anime, if you ask me. I'm surprised more people are talking about it.

[]:             Quote Post by Psychodrake » Thu May 02, 2013 12:13 pm TK421 wrote: It just looks silly, like something out of another (inferior) giant robot anime, if you ask me. I'm surprised more people are talking about it.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu May 02, 2013 2:08 pm I have come to not mind the beast mode stuff because it has become clear by now that NME is not nearly as much about the Evangelions themselves as NGE was. There hasn't been as much of a huge buildup of mystery as to their nature, not like existed in NGE. They really are more like mere tools and weapons this time around. I'm not even convinced that each Eva has its own unique mother-soul anymore; maybe Unit 01 is now a special case.

[]:             Quote Post by Lonecow12 » Tue May 07, 2013 7:02 am Monk Ed wrote: I have come to not mind the beast mode stuff because it has become clear by now that NME is not nearly as much about the Evangelions themselves as NGE was. There hasn't been as much of a huge buildup of mystery as to their nature, not like existed in NGE. They really are more like mere tools and weapons this time around. I'm not even convinced that each Eva has its own unique mother-soul anymore; maybe Unit 01 is now a special case.
